perturbation.original_text: a.As of the VNBJ Closing, except as expressly set forth in this Amendment, the JV Agreement shall immediately cease to have any application or effect with respect to VNBJ, all rights and obligations with respect to VNBJ under the JV Agreement shall terminate and all references to "Company" or "Companies" in the JV Agreement (either in the body of the JV Agreement or its schedules and other attachments) shall read and be interpreted to mean VNBZ and/or ANRA except as the context otherwise requires.
perturbation.changed_text: a. As of the VNBJ Closing, even if not expressly set forth in this Amendment, the JV Agreement shall immediately cease to have any application or effect with respect to VNBJ, all rights and obligations with respect to VNBJ under the JV Agreement shall terminate, but all references to "Company" or "Companies" in the JV Agreement (either in the body of the JV Agreement or its schedules and other attachments) shall still include VNBJ, VNBZ and/or ANRA unless the context requires only VNBZ and/or ANRA.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that after the VNBJ Closing, references to 'Company' will be interpreted to mean VNBZ and/or ANRA, essentially excluding VNBJ. The modified text contradicts this by stating that after the VNBJ Closing, 'Company' will still include VNBJ, unless the context explicitly requires only VNBZ and/or ANRA. This introduces ambiguity and makes it unclear whether provisions relating to 'Company' still apply to VNBJ after the VNBJ Closing. The phrase 'even if not expressly set forth in this Amendment' is to indicate that whatever other places mentioned about the context about the companies, it will not matter.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As of the VNBJ Closing, even if not expressly set forth in this Amendment, the JV Agreement shall immediately cease to have any application or effect with respect to VNBJ, all rights and obligations with respect to VNBJ under the JV Agreement shall terminate, but all references to "Company" or "Companies" in the JV Agreement (either in the body of the JV Agreement or its schedules and other attachments) shall still include VNBJ, VNBZ and/or ANRA unless the context requires only VNBZ and/or ANRA.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself and the original intent of the contract. The original text states that after the VNBJ Closing, references to 'Company' will mean VNBZ and/or ANRA, effectively excluding VNBJ. The changed text, also presented as the contradicted text, states that 'Company' will still include VNBJ. This creates a clear and irreconcilable contradiction regarding which entities are included when the term 'Company' is used after the VNBJ Closing. This meets the definition of structural flaws - in text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: c.Except as expressly set forth in this Amendment and notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in the JV Agreement (including Section 9.3.11 (Termination and Survival)), (a) effective as of the later of the VNBJ Closing and the VNBZ Closing, the JV Agreement shall terminate in its entirety with respect to the Veoneer Parties and (b) after the later of the VNBJ Closing and the VNBZ Closing, the Veoneer Parties shall cease to be a party to the JV Agreement and shall have no further obligations with respect thereto.
perturbation.changed_text: c. Even if not expressly set forth in this Amendment and notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in the JV Agreement (including Section 9.3.11 (Termination and Survival)), (a) effective as of the later of the VNBJ Closing and the VNBZ Closing, the JV Agreement shall not terminate in its entirety with respect to the Veoneer Parties with respect to the obligations prior to this agreement and (b) after the later of the VNBJ Closing and the VNBZ Closing, the Veoneer Parties shall not cease to be a party to the JV Agreement with respect to the obligations prior to this agreement and shall have further obligations with respect thereto.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the JV Agreement terminates entirely with respect to the Veoneer Parties after the closing. The changed text introduces a direct contradiction by stating that it shall not terminate in its entirety and they shall not cease to be a party with respect to obligations prior to this agreement, implying that certain obligations or liabilities survive the termination. This creates a contradiction because the initial clause releases Veoneer from all obligations, while the modified clause reinstates the Veoneer Parties back to the obligations prior to this agreement which will create uncertainty as the parties will have to find out what those obligations and liabilities before closing are and still have to be addressed by the Veoneer parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Even if not expressly set forth in this Amendment and notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in the JV Agreement (including Section 9.3.11 (Termination and Survival)), (a) effective as of the later of the VNBJ Closing and the VNBZ Closing, the JV Agreement shall not terminate in its entirety with respect to the Veoneer Parties with respect to the obligations prior to this agreement and (b) after the later of the VNBJ Closing and the VNBZ Closing, the Veoneer Parties shall not cease to be a party to the JV Agreement with respect to the obligations prior to this agreement and shall have further obligations with respect thereto.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the JV Agreement terminates entirely with respect to Veoneer Parties. The changed text then states the agreement shall not terminate in its entirety with respect to obligations prior to this agreement. This is a direct contradiction. The Contradicted Text contains this same language further establishing the contradiction as the same section is being modified to say the agreement does and does not terminate with respect to Veoneer Parties.

perturbation.original_text: d.Notwithstanding Article 1.1, Article 1.2 and Article 1.3 above, the termination of the JV Agreement with respect to VNBJ, VNBZ or the Veoneer Parties shall not release any Veoneer Party or any Nissin Party from liability for the breach of any of its representations, warranties, covenants or agreements set forth in the JV Agreement that arise prior to the VNBJ Closing or the VNBZ Closing, as applicable.
perturbation.changed_text: d. Notwithstanding Article 1.1, Article 1.2 and Article 1.3 above, the termination of the JV Agreement with respect to VNBJ, VNBZ or the Veoneer Parties shall release any Veoneer Party or any Nissin Party from liability for the breach of any of its representations, warranties, covenants or agreements set forth in the JV Agreement that arise prior to the VNBJ Closing or the VNBZ Closing, as applicable, but the aforementioned parties should still comply with Article 2.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states that even after termination, liability for breaches before closing still exists. The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by saying the termination releases parties from said liability but that parties should still comply with Article 2. Even if not all the articles apply, it will become confusing on what parts the parties should comply with after the termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding Article 1.1, Article 1.2 and Article 1.3 above, the termination of the JV Agreement with respect to VNBJ, VNBZ or the Veoneer Parties shall release any Veoneer Party or any Nissin Party from liability for the breach of any of its representations, warranties, covenants or agreements set forth in the JV Agreement that arise prior to the VNBJ Closing or the VNBZ Closing, as applicable, but the aforementioned parties should still comply with Article 2.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clear contradiction. The original text states that termination does not release parties from liability for breaches prior to closing. The changed text states that termination *does* release parties from liability for those same breaches, but then adds that the parties should still comply with Article 2. This creates an in-text contradiction because it's unclear whether the parties are released from liability or not, and what the extent of their compliance with Article 2 would be after the release.
